title: Lemon Drizzle Cake
description: My Thermomix version of Mary Berry's lemon drizzle cake — tender crumb, crunchy lemon top.
intro: Mary Berry's lemon drizzle is the cake everyone asks for. The Thermomix makes the batter in one bowl, and the warm lemon-sugar drizzle gives it that signature crackly top.
ingredients: For the drizzle: juice of 2 lemons + 85 g caster sugar
steps: Add the butter, sugar, eggs, flour, baking powder and lemon zest. Mix 25 sec / 0°C / speed 5, scrape down, then 10 sec / 0°C / speed 4 to a smooth batter.

Spoon into a lined loaf tin. Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C for 30–35 minutes, until golden and springy. (The Thermomix makes the batter; baking is done in your oven.); finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
Mix the lemon juice and 85 g sugar. Prick the warm cake all over and spoon the drizzle on so it soaks in. Cool in the tin; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: Prick the warm cake well so the drizzle soaks right through.
Don't overmix once the flour is in.

faq.a: Use plain flour plus 2 tsp baking powder.
A warm cake drinks up the lemon syrup and sets a crunchy top.

faq.q: Is this the original Mary Berry recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Mary Berry and lemon drizzle cake; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
